item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded:
Thank you! I had a lot of fun with it. Less fun was trying to keep it from being long but keep the characterization going, but I'm happy it worked out in the end.

Brendamat OTP indeed.

Hope you like my other stuff!

(Would give +5, one for each head)

Hell(s), this is good! I've found that a lot of SCP Articles that are really good also tend to be really long, which I don't mind, but it'd nice to have a lovely shorter article. It plays with the idea of Thoughtforms in a way that ties the article in well with the SCP Universe while also providing a very un-SCP story. (Branda/Tiamat OTP)
Now I'm gonna go check out more of your stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That post immeasurably improved my experience of posting this article, it is a treasure.

I'm glad you liked the article, and identified with poor Drs. Corbin and Nass. Drafting is a harsh mistress. I'm a big fan of thoughtforms; my favourite thing on the site is S&C Plastics, which probably has a lot to do with that.

Thanks for the feedback!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this was the post that brought me to this article, and im really glad knowing that all this was gonna happen didnt spoil my enjoyment of it :p

i love the meta nature of the format screw, it made me chuckle quite sensibly as someone who is perpetually stuck in Drafting and Redrafting limbo. i also really enjoyed this article as an exploration of a thoughtform! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: HOT GIRL SHIT

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What does this mean??????

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: That's a lovely way of looking at it; of course I baked a lot of that intentionality into what I wrote, but the final metaphor escaped me until now. Thanks :D

This is the perfect expression of what it's like to try to write an article as a novice. After having it torn down and building it back up over and over again, eventually you finally get to see your five-headed dragon monster take to the skies of the site.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: THANK YOU! Sometimes it's possible to squeeze extra notes out of what starts as a one-note joke :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not only could I not do this without the D&D reference, it made image searching easier - not many people draw Babylonian goddesses, but lots of people have D&D miniatures!
TIAMAT IS TIAMAT. SUPER COOL!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I admit I fell in love the moment Tiamat mentioned vaugely remembering being a five-headed dragon. The rest of the piece brought it home though (with only enough redundancy to clue in people who aren't D&D nerds)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Appreciate the kind words! "Format screw: editor keeps making actual Tiamat Tiamat-class for no apparent reason" was the entirety of my initial idea, glad it somehow became more.

Thanks :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! They were a ton of fun to do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent work! The minor format screw is inventive and fun. Super good stuff dude!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the conversation between nass and corbin was a really creative part of this article and is a big reason for my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I'm glad that delay worked as a device; it's there because I needed logs of stuff that would get Brenda sent to detention to show up in the file in a way that wouldn't get her sent to detention, thus preventing the climax lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love the formatting you did with the back and forth editing conversation. Also thought it was a nice twist with the delay on the edits. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The canonical interpretation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fully reasonable views, of course. In the end, I sacrifice whatever I have to sacrifice to make the story work the way I want it to. The extent to which it works for each reader is totally a personal decision, and I certainly have no problem with that! Thanks for leaving your thoughts, I appreciate it. (In terms of justification: in my mind, the fact that nobody actually thought this thing was anything but a pile of bones meant that Brenda's chat with Tiamat didn't call down the reprisal it probably ought to have. Pretty thin? Maybe.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am really teetering on the edge of an upvote for this one. To be clear, I really like the story itself, but…

The one problem I have with this is that I just can't see the Foundation letting any of this happen. I can buy the doctors being sassy as all hell, because, they kinda work in an actual hellscape and humour is a major coping mechanism. The problem I do have is that the Foundation isn't incompetent and is very strict about following the rules (at least in my headcannon). That whole section charter quote, in my headcannon, is not a slap on the wrist kind of violation (did she even get that much?). For me, that would be grounds for immediate and pretty severe disciplinary action.

As I said, I like the story, so it's not even close to a downvote, but this just really messes with how I view the Foundation (and not in a good way). Ah well, I hope this opinion isn't completely outlandish to you. I look forward to seeing more (and hopefully upvoting next time)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, I tried to do something different and… boy, it seems to have paid off. Glad you liked it :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the first SCP I've read in a while, and it's awesome. The whole back-and-forth thing is something I haven't seen before, but it was a great compliment to an already fascinating article. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! It's been a busy thread :P

My very first.

Thank you all very much for reading <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All the praise I could give has already been given, so I'm just gonna say I really, really enjoyed this. Cheers. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's a fun interpretation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was SOOO expecting even the last couple events were a fabrication/vandalism to give more power to Tiamat, and that the belief of the Foundation isn't enough to break out of containment, just come back from the dead, maybe as a small harmless lizard. That was weirdly specific of my imagination.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks! I had fun with it.

Fixed the links; looks like CC Search changed their web address some time after my licensing was confirmed. Appreciate the heads-up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this SCP! This is a very stylish idea. +1
Also, the link of the image except Wikimedia is broken.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Im in love with this SCP +1, fantastic job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am bad at expressing "legitimately touched," because I'm so instinctively sarcastic. Rest assured, irrespective of my inability to state it properly, that I very deeply appreciate the compliment. Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
harry, you are beginning to become one of my favorite authors on this site. your dialogue here, as always, is wonderful. and the way you develop the SCP into a very real threat is extremely compelling.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love me a good mythological SCP, especially when it's this interesting!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks! I do think the confession is a bit heavy, but the hints throughout just weren't doing it for some reviewers so I decided to err on the side of caution. I have faith in the readers, but I also have faith in people getting irritated if they don't get what happened, so I split the difference.

Always appreciate the read, and the response!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For the most part I enjoyed this enough to upvote. The characterization of Nass and Corbin was nice, albeit somewhat quick. The conversations between 5866 and Corbin was probably my favorite part of this. They bounced off of each other nicely and the dialogue from 5866 had that ancient being flair while not straying far from modern speech patterns.

My main issue with this is the confession log. It feels a bit exposition-dumpy to me. I know that Corbin’s plan had to be explained else 5866 reanimating wouldn’t have made sense, but it felt convenient that she just spills everything out in three paragraphs. Maybe that’s a personal thing and I can’t think of an alternate way you could have potentially done the reveal.

Regardless, I had fun reading this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought about responding with a quip, but I couldn't think of one. I must've used them all up in the article.

Appreciate hearing your reason, and of course it's a legit matter of taste. I'll take the faint praise and move on :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is eventually revealed to be an actually pretty interesting idea. Unfortunately, about half of the article prior to the reveal is quipping, which really did not work for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll think about it; I don't see a need personally, although the approach you're suggesting certainly has merit. I honestly think it's teetering on the edge of being freighted with too much character as it stands, since people weren't getting what Brenda's deal was and I had to really emphasize it. I think the structure will start to creak if I put anything else on, and I don't want to take anything off. I certainly respect your opinion on this, though, and I will give it some thought.

Thanks for the ideas, something to chew on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the interactions between Brenda and Tiamat, but the in universe commentary between the two scientists needs to be redone. It starts flat and is only hurt more by how much time you spend lampshading your own esoteric class. The payoff that each argument makes the goddess more real is only made less satisfying as a result, especially because those arguments keep interrupting the interviews. You should refocus their debate on some aspect of 5866 that actually feels worth arguing about and reinforcing in universe; it would make the jailbreak more cathartic and could even give me something to remember about Brenda’s colleague.

the hell is going on

Edit: jinxed it

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Thanks, I just kept thinking of more and more things like that, and every time I thought "someone's gonna call me on all these inside jokes" I also thought "who fucking cares, I like it." And then nobody did call me on it, so I guess occasionally, in selected circumstances, I think the right thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent take, excellent comment, thank you :P
I enjoyed the in-universe parallel to some of the writing advice on the wiki, and the style it was done in was really fun to read!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent characters, excellent way to play off of a containment class. +1

item.title: Re: Highly positive +1
item.content:encoded: And here's my © Thanks so much! and my ® I'm glad you liked it! and my just plain clichéd :) to indicate my appreciation for the comment, and the vote. Y'all are the wind beneath my dragon wings.

item.title: Highly positive +1
item.content:encoded: This has the trademark HarryBlank energy in the best possible way. From the formatting, to the story, I love this to bits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Third, that's the only relationship possible with puns.

Second, awesome :) Goal achieved.

First, thank you very much indeed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First up, Harry you are on a roll.

Second, the disputes feel real, with some good comedic undertones.

And third, I have a love-hate relationship with those puns.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, I tried real hard :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That last image is rad

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks partner :P Got quite the backlog, although I've nearly depleted it. Only missing one class with an inbuilt ACS logo, and it won't be up for… oh, months.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, you're really are taking on all the esoteric classes. Glad to see you're still going strong!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly I think I could end my whole posting spree on that one-liner and be happy with it as my legacy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really love the back and forth in this, giving us a window into the sort of disputes are bound to happen with in universe classifications and what not… They MUST as people are always arguing about it IRL so it follows that they would do so in the actual Foundation.

But mostly I just like that there's an excuse for the wordplay concerning the naming or "classification" of this snake goddess.

As always, ends on a terrific pay off one-liner

item.title: The Make-A-Wish Foundation
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to claygolem, EstrellaYoshte, Grigori Karpin and Ralliston for their crit!

This was supposed to be titled "The Namesake," but I just had to open my big mouth to Grigori and mention that I was tempted to call it "The Namesnake," and then he wouldn't let me back off on it. If a pun passes Grigori's filter, I know it must be good.

Neither Dr. Michael Nass, who is fictional, nor HarryBlank, which is a pseudonym, wrote the Wikipedia article on Tiamat. That was, however, actually a quote from it, which can be found (in the lede, amusingly enough) here.
